ņØĖļ▓żĒåĀļ”¼ņĢäņØ┤Ēģ£ņ×ÉļÅÖļ│æĒĢ®ŌöāInventory Slots Auto-Sorting
GameSetting/AutomaticInventoryItemRestockŌöāWhen there are multiple consumables of the same type in different inventory slots, items will be consumed starting from the lowest priority slot.\nRegardless of the hotkey used, slots positioned further to the bottom right are considered lower priority.\nExample: If there's Steak in Slots 1, 3, and 5, pressing any hotkey for these slots will consume the Steak from slot 5 first.
ņśĄņģś/VLSņāüĒśĖņ×æņÜ®ĒÖĢņ×źļ¦ĄĒÖ£ņä▒ĒÖöŌöāEnable Expanded Map for VLS

ņØĖļ▓żĒåĀļ”¼ņĢäņØ┤Ēģ£ņÜ░ņäĀņé¼ņÜ®ņäżņĀĢŌöāItem Consumption Priority Settings
GameSetting/InventoryItemUsePriorityŌöāWhen there are multiple consumables of the same type in different inventory slots, they will be consumed according to the designated settings, regardless of the assigned hotkey.\nThe top-left inventory slot is Slot 1, and the bottom-right one is Slot 10.\nExample:\nAscending:  If there's Steak in Slots 1, 3, and 5, pressing any hotkey for these slots will consume the Steak from slot 1 first.\nDescending: If there's Steak in Slots 1, 3, and 5, pressing any hotkey for these slots will consume the Steak from Slot 5 first.

ņØīņŗØņ×ÉļÅÖņäŁņĘ©ņäżņĀĢ/ļ¬¼ņŖżĒä░ņĀäĒł¼ņżæ/ĒÜīļ│Ąļ¤ēĻĖ░ņżĆŌöāRegen from Food: During Combat with Monsters

SeasonCollection/Info/TitleŌöāSeason Collection
SeasonCollection/Info/DescŌöāWhen you use a Season Fragments Bundle obtained from Daily Missions, the Store, and other sources, you'll receive Season Fragments and added to your Season Collection.\nCollect all Season Fragments to complete the collection and claim your reward!
SeasonCollection/Tooltip/UnregisteredŌöāUse Exchange Fragments to get Season Fragments.
SeasonCollection/Reward/TitleŌöāSeason Collection Rewards
SeasonCollection/Reward/GradeŌöāLevel {0} Reward
SeasonCollection/Reward/ConditionŌöāAvailable after claiming reward from previous level
SeasonCollection/Point/TitleŌöāSeason Fragment Points
SeasonCollection/Point/Info/1Ōöā*You can spend Exchange Fragments to use Season Fragments.
SeasonCollection/Point/Info/2Ōöā*If you obtain a Season Fragment that you've already used, it will be converted into a certain amount of Season Fragment Points based on its rarity.(Uncommon:10Pt / Common:1Pt)
SeasonCollection/Point/Info/3Ōöā*At the end of the season, any unused Exchange Fragments and Season Fragment Points will be reset.
SeasonCollection/Popup/Confirm/TitleŌöāUse Season Fragments
SeasonCollection/Popup/ConfirmŌöāSpend 1 Exchange Fragment to use the selected Season Fragment?
SeasonCollection/Popup/RejectŌöāNot enough Exchange Fragments.
SeasonCollection/Popup/ExchangeŌöāYou've already obtained this Season Fragment. It has been converted to {0} Season Fragment Points.
SeasonCollection/Popup/NotYetProgressŌöāThis level is not available yet. Check your current Season Collection level in progress.
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Title/1ŌöāSeason Collection
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Info/1/1Ōöā*Collect Season Fragments to complete the Season Collection and get rewards.
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Info/1/2Ōöā*You must complete the current level of the Season Collection to unlock a new one.
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Info/1/3Ōöā*Fragments collected in your current level are not accumulated when they're duplicated, instead they're converted into Exchange Points.
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Title/2ŌöāHow to Get Season Fragments
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Info/2Ōöā*You can get Season Fragments through different means such as gameplay rewards, daily missions, login events, personal store, daily boxes, etc.
SeasonCollection/SeasonPackCount/TitleŌöāMy Season Fragment Vouchers
SeasonCollection/SeasonPackCount/InfoŌöā*You can open Season Fragment Vouchers in Forging. Click on them to go there.
SeasonCollection/SeasonPackCount/TooltipŌöāMove to Forging to open your Season Fragment Vouchers.
SeasonCollection/Notice/CompleteŌöāYou've completed level {0} of the Season Collection.\nCheck rewards!

SeasonCollection/Completed/TitleŌöāLevel {0} Completed

DualModule/Tooltip/TitleŌöāDual Module
DualModule/Tooltip/InfoŌöāThe module effect is <color=#FFFFFF>automatically applied</color> when a <color=#FFFFFF>Skin Data Box</color> is opened and you receive a <color=#D05EFF>Complete Epic skin</color>.\nThis effect does not apply to Complete Epic Skins received through Research or Events. \n(Dual Modules are consumed per Epic Skin. If you donŌĆÖt have enough, the effects are applied in the order the skins appear.)\n\n<color=#FFFFFF>[Dual Module Effects]</color>\nAn <color=#FFFFFF>Additional Complete Epic skin</color> is offered and you can <color=#FFFFFF>select</color> 1 out of the 2 Epic skins.
DualModule/SkinSelect/InfoŌöāSelect the skin you'd like to receive.
CharacterGuide/1/OneHandSwordŌöā<color=orange>Daggers</color> are powerful for instant attacks. You can disappear for a moment and reappear again with the Cloak and Dagger skill to deal cellular damage.
CharacterGuide/1/TwoHandSwordŌöā<color=orange>Two-handed Swords</color> are weaker than other weapons in the first half of the match but get really powerful in the second half. The weapon skill Parry can block the enemy's main attack once, so it becomes even scarier in the second half.
CharacterGuide/1/AxeŌöā<color=orange>Axes</color> are stronger in the earlier stages of the game. The weapon skill, Maleficent Helix, allows you to deal extra damage and heal after getting stacks from basic attacks.
CharacterGuide/1/DualSwordŌöā<color=orange>Dual Swords</color> are kind of hidden weapons. You cannot choose them from the start - you have to make twin swords first, using a kitchen knife and a longsword. The specialty of having two swords is being able to perform fast attacks, and although their attack power is low, they are most effective when paired with the Dual Sword Rampage weapon skill and Adrenaline Burst (W), which maximize the life steal effect.
CharacterGuide/2/PistolŌöā<color=orange>Pistols</color> do not have a long range but they provide good survivability. The pistol weapon skill, Moving Reload, increases the movement speed of the user for a moment while reloading ammo.
CharacterGuide/2/SniperRifleŌöā<color=orange>Sniper</color>s require good use of telephoto cameras and traps in order to take advantage of the vision range and long distance of the Sniper Rifles. If Aya succeeds in predicting her enemy's movements, she can hit them at a very long distance with the Sniper skill, Ballistic Advantage.
CharacterGuide/2/AssaultRifleŌöā<color=orange>Assault Rifles</color> most important feature is that their basic attack fires 3 round bursts. Your movement will be restricted for the 3 round bursts. Remember to activate the weapon skill for even MORE damage!
CharacterGuide/3/RapierŌöā<color=orange>Rapiers</color> can quickly attack the enemy, dealing even more damage if you perform a powerful critical blow. Quickly charge toward the enemy with the weapon skill Quick Cut, closing in on the enemy and dealing damage.
CharacterGuide/3/TwoHandSwordŌöā<color=orange>Two-hand Swords</color> are weaker than other weapons in the first half of the match but get really powerful in the second half. The weapon skill Parry can block the enemy's main attack once, so it becomes even scarier in the second half.
CharacterGuide/3/SpearŌöā<color=orange>Spears</color> are powerful in middle range, but you need to control your distance well as they are quite weak when fighting close range. The weapon skill Shadow Stab has a knockback effect, in addition to movement speed reduction, so you can maintain distance when fighting.
CharacterGuide/4/HammerŌöā<color=orange>Hammers</color> are weapons with strong attack power. The Armor Piercing weapon skill has a defense power reduction effect, so you can quickly suppress enemies with low defense power.
CharacterGuide/4/BatŌöā<color=orange>Bats</color> have an advantage by being faster. By using the Full Swing skill, you can stun the enemy - by using it with Heavy Strike (E), Magnus can become very scary when fighting against an enemy between four walls.
CharacterGuide/5/HighAngleFireŌöā<color=orange>Throwing weapons</color> are weapons more suited for basic attacks than shurikens. Smokescreen, the weapon skill, reduces the enemy's movement speed, so you can reduce the distance between them and increase your chances of landing Zahir's skill shots.
CharacterGuide/5/DirectFireŌöā<color=orange>Shuriken</color> have tricky recipes for high tier weapons compared to throwing weapons. The weapon skill, Caltrops, stays on the ground for a long time, allowing for more favorable fights by positioning yourself based on where your opponent is coming from or where you're headed.
CharacterGuide/6/BowŌöā<color=orange>Bows</color> have rather low attack power, but fast attack speed. The weapon skill Rain of Arrows is hard to master because of the delay it has, but you can deal powerful damage and slow down your enemies if you get it right.
CharacterGuide/6/CrossBowŌöā<color=orange>Crossbows</color> are weapons with powerful attack power. The weapon skill Expulsion Shot pushes back the enemies, so you can keep distance from them when fighting.
CharacterGuide/7/GloveŌöā<color=orange>Gloves</color> have fast attack speed, which is good when combined with Stomping Foot (Q) and the Uppercut skill, allowing him to attack continuously.
CharacterGuide/7/TonfaŌöā<color=orange>Tonfas</color> are weapons with high defense power. You can block the enemy's main attack by using Bluff (W) and the weapon skill Quick Spin.
CharacterGuide/8/GuitarŌöā<color=orange>Love &...</color> prevents enemies from attacking or moving, allowing you a one-sided fight. It also causes enemies hit to move toward the source of the released the sound wave, making it an efficient weapon skill to reduce distance between you and an enemy attempting to flee.

ņ£Āļŗłņś© ļīĆņĀä ļ×ŁĒü¼ ņäżļ¬ģ ļé┤ņÜ®Ōöā3 members of your team can participate in a Union Circuit match. Your tier is determined based on match results.\n\n<b>[Rules for Promotions & Demotions]</b>\n- Union Circuit consists of tiers ranging from F to SSS.\n- Final rankings are determined based on score points (team placement + TK), not just team placement.\n- Promotions and demotions are based on your final rankings that are derived from your score points.\n\n<b>[F to A Tiers]</b>\n- Tiers from F to A are determined by your points and each match has an entry cost that is deducted from your points.\n\n<b>[S to SSS Tiers]</b>\n- Matching in S to SSS tiers requires tickets specific to those tiers.

SquadRumbleManagementSystemŌöāHow does Union Circuit work?
SquadRumble_Introduce01ŌöāYou need 1 ticket to join a match. The ticket for your current highest tier is used first.
SquadRumble_Introduce02ŌöāIn Union Circuit, final rankings are determined by score, not survival placement.
SquadRumble_Introduce03ŌöāTickets are distributed as rewards after the match fully ends and scores are finalized.
SquadRumble_Introduce04ŌöāYour Union will either be promoted, demoted, or remain the same according to the final rankings.\n(Promotion conditions vary by tier.)

Ļ░ĆņŻĮ ņĢäņØ┤Ēģ£ ĒÜŹļōØŌöāGrab <color=white>Leather</color> off of Wild Animals!\n \nThere's a lot of items that need leather. 
Ļ┐Ć ņĢäņØ┤Ēģ£ ĒÜŹļōØŌöāYou can grab <color=white>Honey</color> from some boxes around Lumia Island.\n\nYou can find the location of certain items in the catalog.
Ļ┐ĆĒāä ņÜ░ņ£Ā ņĢäņØ┤Ēģ£ ĒÜŹļōØŌöāYou can make <color=#35FF94>Honey Milk</color> by combining... <color=white>Milk</color> and <color=white>Honey</color>!\n\nFind <color=white>Milk</color> and <color=white>Honey</color> in boxes around Lumia Island and try making <color=#35FF94>Honey Milk</color>.

ņÜ┤ņäØ ņĢäņØ┤Ēģ£ ĒÜŹļōØŌöāMeteorites impact once a day using meteorite guidance technology.\n\nA Meteorite will impact from Day 2 to Day 4 in a random area. The impact location is announced a minute before it occurs.\n\nAfter the <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color> impacts, you can collect it - stay nearby if you need it!
ņāØļ¬ģņØś ļéśļ¼┤ ņĢäņØ┤Ēģ£ ĒÜŹļōØŌöāSome trees have accumulated VF (Vital Force) over time. You can collect <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color>, also known as the World Tree, and harness its power!\n\nA Tree of Life spawns on Day 2 and Day 3, and an announcement occurs 1 minute before it spawns.\n
VFĒśłņĢĪņāśĒöī ņĢäņØ┤Ēģ£ ĒÜŹļōØŌöāVF (Vital Force) is theorized to be the cause of physically impossible phenomena.\n\nHumans "infused" with VF have been found to have various abilities and powerful regenerative abilities. Experiments testing this were conducted on Lumia Island. \n\nWickeline drops a <color=#D05EFF>VF Blood Sample</color> when slain. She appears on Night 4 and roams around Lumia Island.
ĒÅ¼ņŖżņĮöņ¢┤ ņĢäņØ┤Ēģ£ ĒÜŹļōØŌöā<color=#D05EFF>Force Cores</color> are from a natural ore enriched with VF. It can be used to replicate items and weapons from legends and myths.\n\nCombine <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color> and <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color> to make a <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color>, or get it from Omega.\n\n
ĒöīļĀłņØ┤ņ¢┤/ņĢ╝ņāØļÅÖļ¼╝ Ēö╝ĒĢ┤ ņŻ╝ĻĖ░ŌöāBefore you start fighting, get stronger first.\n\nPrioritize building your weapon, and then attack your enemies and build your weapon mastery!\n
ļŗŁ ņ▓śņ╣śĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāChickens are the weakest Wild Animal. \nSlaying chickens will drop <color=white>Leather</color>, and if you're lucky, <color=white>Meat</color> and <color=white>Feathers</color>.\n\nSlay Chickens to obtain materials and use them to craft equipment and food.
ļ░ĢņźÉ ņ▓śņ╣śĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāBats spawn 30 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Bat will drop a <color=white>Telephoto Camera</color> and <color=white>Leather</color>, which will give you vision for a limited time.\n\nSlayed Bats will reappear in the same spot after 160 seconds.
ļ®¦ļÅ╝ņ¦Ć ņ▓śņ╣śĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāBoars appear 60 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Boar will drop two chunks of <color=white>Meat</color> and <color=white>Leather</color>, but be careful of the boar's charge attack!\n\n\nSlayed Boars will reappear in the same spot after 150 seconds.
ļōżĻ░£ ņ▓śņ╣śĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāWild Dogs appear 30 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Wild Dog will drop <color=white>Leather</color> and a <color=white>Common Material Item</color>, but beware that their first attack will reduce your healing!\n\nSlayed Wild Dogs will reappear in the same spot after 160 seconds.
ļŖæļīĆ ņ▓śņ╣śĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāWolves appear 90 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Wolf will drop <color=white>Meat</color>, <color=white>Leather</color>, and one <color=#35FF94>Uncommon Material Item</color>.\n\nWolves of Level 10 or above will sometimes drop <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color> or <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color>.\nBe carefulŌĆöWolves like to roam in groups of two, and they can call their friends to joint attack!\n\nSlayed Wolves will reappear in the same spot after 190 seconds.
Ļ│░ ņ▓śņ╣śĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāBears appear 150 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Bear will drop <color=white>Meat</color>, <color=white>Leather</color>, and one <color=#35FF94>Uncommon Material Item</color>.\n\nBears of Level 7 and above will sometimes drop <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color>, <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color>, <color=#D05EFF>Mythril</color>, and <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color>.\nBe careful as the bear will stun you if you take a hit.\n\n\nSlayed Bears will reappear in the same spot after 250 seconds.
ņ£äĒü┤ļØ╝ņØĖ ļ░Ģņé¼ ņ▓śņ╣śĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāWickeline is believed to have been involved in earlier experiments in some way, but nobody is still around to confirm it.\n\nShe comes out from the Research Center and starts to move around Lumia Island at the beginning of Night 4.\n\nShe is tough, but drops some goodies: a <color=#D05EFF>VF Blood Sample</color> and additional item rewards! \n\nKill her and you'll also get an "Enhanced by Vital Force" buff for 3 minutes.\n\nSubjects Enhanced by Vital Force cause extra damage over 3 seconds when they attack. \n\nWickeline is strong, but is worth the risk to take down.
ņé¼ļāź ņłÖļĀ©ļÅä ļŗ¼ņä▒ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāHunt Wild Animals to increase your Hunt Mastery.\n\nYou can also increase Hunt Mastery when dealing damage to or taking damage from Wild Animals.\n\nYou will deal more damage to Wild Animals as your Hunt Mastery goes up.
ņĀ£ņ×æ ņłÖļĀ©ļÅä ļŗ¼ņä▒ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāCraft items to increase Craft Mastery.\n\nYou can increase Craft Mastery when crafting Weapons, Head Armor, Chest Armor, Arm/Accesory Armor, Leg Armor, and Food.\n\nOut of combat HP Regen will increase as your Crafting Mastery goes up.
ĒāÉņāē ņłÖļĀ©ļÅä ļŗ¼ņä▒ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāExplore Lumia Island to increase your Search Mastery.\n\nYou can increase Search Mastery by opening item boxes, opening air supplies, collecting resources, and operating security consoles.
ņØ┤ļÅÖ ņłÖļĀ©ļÅä ļŗ¼ņä▒ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāMove around the island to increase your Movement Mastery.\n\nTraveling far, moving to new areas, and crafting Leg Armor all also increase your mastery.\n\nYour Movement Speed will increase as your Movement Mastery goes up.
ņ▓┤ļĀź ņłÖļĀ©ļÅä ļŗ¼ņä▒ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāIncrease your Health Mastery!\n\nYour Health Mastery is increased when you make food or take damage.
ļ░®ņ¢┤ ņłÖļĀ©ļÅä ļŗ¼ņä▒ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāIncrease your Defense Mastery by taking damage! Just don't die. \n\nYou can increase your Defense Mastery by crafting Head Armor and taking damage from players, wild animals, or traps.\n\nWhen your Defense Mastery goes up, you take less damage.
ļ¬ģņāü ņłÖļĀ©ļÅä ļŗ¼ņä▒ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāIncrease your Meditation Mastery!\n\nYou can increase your Meditation Mastery by using SP or crafting drinks.\n\nWhen your Meditation Mastery goes up, so does your SP.
ĒĢ©ņĀĢ ņłÖļĀ©ļÅä ļŗ¼ņä▒ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāIncrease your Trap Mastery by actively using traps.\n\nYou can increase your Trap Mastery by crafting traps, setting traps, or dealing damage with traps.\n\nThe higher your Trap Mastery, the more damage your traps do.
ļ¼┤ĻĖ░ ņłÖļĀ©ļÅä ļŗ¼ņä▒ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāIncrease your Weapon Mastery by attacking enemies.\n\nIncrease your Weapon Mastery by dealing damage to or killing players and Wild Animals, or by crafting weapons.\n\nYour Basic Attack and Skill Damage will increase as your Weapon Mastery goes up.
ņĢīĒīī ņ▓śņ╣śĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāAlpha is an Android powered by VF (Vital Force).\n\nAlpha appears on Night 2 in two random areas.\n\nAlpha drops <color=#D05EFF>Mythril</color> and <color=#35FF94>Recon Drone</color> when it is killed.
ņśżļ®öĻ░Ć ņ▓śņ╣śĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāOmega is an Android that is powered by VF (Vital Force).\n\nOmega appears on Night 3. It will not spawn in the same area as Alpha.\n\nOmega uses special abilities from its VF, but often becomes unstable, revealing its weakness whenever it takes over a certain amount of damage.\n\nOmega drops <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color> and <color=#2AC0FF>EMP Drone</color> when it is killed.
ļŻ©ļ»ĖņĢä Ēü¼ļĀłļö¦ ĒÜŹļōØĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāThe <color=#F4FF63>Credit</color> is a currency used on Lumia Island as part of an experiment.\n\nPlayers start the game with some Credits. Throughout the game, players earn Credits upon each day/night change, killing an enemy test subject, or slaying Wild or Mutant Animals.\n\n<color=#F4FF63>Credits</color> are used to call in Remote Drones or use Kiosks.
ļŻ©ļ»ĖņĢä ņĀäņåĪļō£ļĪĀ ņé¼ņÜ®ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāRemote Drones were originally stealth surveillance drones used to track test subjects on Lumia Island.\n\nSince then, researchers have given these drones the ability to deliver materials to test subjects who pay for it with <color=#F4FF63>Credits</color>!\n\nNot having good box RNG? Is your required material only left in restricted areas? Leave it to a Transfer Drone!
ļŻ©ļ»ĖņĢä ņĀäņåĪņĮśņåö ņé¼ņÜ®ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāResearchers have placed Kiosks across Lumia Island to intensify the results of their experiments.\nAt a Kiosk, test subjects can pay <color=#F4FF63>Credits</color> to call in a Transfer Supply Box.\n
ņĮöļ░£ĒŖĖ ļ¬©ļō£ ņŖ╣ļ”¼ĒĢśĻĖ░ŌöāTry out Cobalt Protocol, the second mode in Eternal Return!\n\nIf you need a break from crafting and farming, take on the fast-paced combat of Cobalt Protocol! Also, you won't have to worry about placement.\nAchieve victory by depleting the enemy team's Life Score!

HOW WE USE YOUR DATA\n Nimble Neuron collects your data so that we can:\n - Deliver the Game to you. \n - Comply with our legal obligations, legally binding orders or requests. \n - Identify your account and provide you with customer support.\n - Answer your questions that you inquired through our customer support.\n - Provide technical support such as bug fixes and patches, improve our Game service.\n - Provide technical protection against usage of unauthorized programs or improper gameplay that could harm Game users.\n - Track usage patterns and generate statistics, such as a leaderboard and usage, from service use.\n - Send or distribute a prize related to an event/promotion. HOW WE STORE YOUR DATA\n4.1 Generally, your data will be stored until purpose of collecting such data is achieved. Nimble Neuron securely stores your data in accordance with appropriate technical measures. However, due to unforeseen circumstances, Nimble Neuron does not guarantee loss, misuse, or alternation of your data by third parties.\n\n5. RETENTION PERIODS\n5.1 We will not store or keep your personal data for a longer period than is necessary in light of the purposes for which we process them. Only where we are legally obliged to, or where this is necessary for defending our interests in the context of judicial proceedings, we will store the personal data for longer periods. \n\n6. INTERNATIONAL TRANSFER\n6.1 Your personal data may be transferred outside the European Union, your countries, or regions that you are located. If your personal data is transferred, we will take all reasonable steps and implement the necessary data transfer mechanisms, such as the Standard Contractual Clauses adopted and approved by the European Commission, to protect any personal data are treated securely in accordance with this Privacy Policy and relying on our GameŌĆÖs hosting service, Amazon Web Services. \n\n7. YOUR DATA PROTECTION RIGHTS\n7.1 Nimble Neuron would like to make sure you are fully aware of all of your data protection rights. Every user is entitled to the following:\n ŌĆō The right to access: You have the right to request Nimble Neuron for copies of your personal data. We may charge you a small fee for this service.\n ŌĆō The right to rectification: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron correct any information you believe is inaccurate. You also have the right to request Nimble Neuron to complete the information you believe is incomplete.\n ŌĆō The right to erasure: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron erase your personal data, under certain conditions.\n ŌĆō The right to restrict processing: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron restrict the processing of your personal data, under certain conditions.\n ŌĆō The right to object to processing: You have the right to object to Nimble NeuronŌĆÖs processing of your personal data, under certain conditions.\n ŌĆō The right to data portability: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron transfer the data that we have collected to another organization, or directly to you, under certain conditions.\n If you make a request, we have one month to respond to you. In some cases, however, particularly if your request is more complex, more time may be required up to a maximum of three months after the data on which we received the request. In this case you will, of course, be kept fully informed of our progress. If you would like to exercise any of these rights, please contact us at our email: eternalreturn@nimbleneuron.com.\n\n8. COOKIES\n8.1 Cookies are text files placed on your computer to collect standard Internet log information and visitor behavior information. When you visit our websites, we may collect information from you automatically through cookies or similar technology. For further information, visit allaboutcookies.org.\n8.2 Nimble Neuron uses cookies in a range of ways to improve your experience on our website, including:\n ŌĆō Keeping you signed in.\n ŌĆō Understanding how you use our website.\n ŌĆō Provide you with personalized preferences setting.\n8.3 There are a number of different types of cookies, however, our website uses:\n ŌĆō Functionality: Nimble Neuron may use these cookies so that we recognize you on our website and remember your previously selected preferences. These could include what language you prefer and location you are in. A mix of first-party and third-party cookies are used.\n ŌĆō Advertising: Nimble Neuron may use these cookies to collect information about your visit to our website, the content you viewed, the links you followed and information about your browser, device, and your IP address. Nimble Neuron may share limited aspects of this data with third parties for advertising purposes. Nimble Neuron may also share online data collected through cookies with our advertising partners. This means that when you visit another website, you may be shown advertising based on your browsing patterns on our website.\n8.4 You can set your browser not to accept cookies, and the above website tells you how to remove cookies from your browser. However, in a few cases, some of our website features may not function as a result.\n \n9. PRIVACY POLICIES OF OTHER WEBSITE\n9.1 The Nimble Neuron website(s) contains links to other websites. Our privacy policy applies only to our website, so if you click on a link to another website, you should read their privacy policy.\n\n10. CHANGES TO OUR PRIVACY POLICY\n10.1 Nimble Neuron keeps its privacy policy under regular review and places any updates on this web page. You will be deemed to have accepted such updates by continuing to use Nimble NeuronŌĆÖs service. This privacy policy was last updated on January 22th, 2026.\n\n11. YOUR PRIVACY RIGHTS\n11.1 In certain regions, you have a right to report a complaint or if you have concerns about how we process your personal data. Please contact your local authority for more information.\n\n12. DATA PROTECTION OFFICER\n12.1 Nimble NeuronŌĆÖs data protection officer is Joong-gon Lee (contact: eternalreturn@nimbleneuron.com).\n\n13. HOW TO CONTACT US\n13.1 If you have any questions about Nimble NeuronŌĆÖs privacy policy, the data we hold on you, or you would like to exercise one of your data protection rights, please contact us at: eternalreturn@nimbleneuron.com.

Please visit ZendeskŌĆÖs privacy policy at https://www.zendesk.com/company/customers-partners/privacy-policy, and GeekNationŌĆÖs privacy policy at https://geeknation.info/privacy-policy/.\n (ii) Information collected automatically: IP address, internet service provider, country code, computer operating system, hardware information, application software, country, game language, chat log.\n (iii) Information collected from third parties: If the Game is distributed and payment is handled by Microsoft, its software distribution platform may collect your account information, along with payment information from you. Please review the Microsoft Terms of Sale for more information: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/storedocs/terms-of-sale. \n \n 2. This information shall be deleted once such event/promotion is complete and the prize(s) is received by the winner(s). \n \n 3. HOW WE STORE YOUR DATA\n 3.1 Generally, your data will be stored until purpose of collecting such data is achieved. Nimble Neuron securely stores your data in accordance with appropriate technical measures. Skill/LobbyDesc/1044500Ōöā<color=green>Passive:</color> EchionŌĆÖs <color=white>VF Gauge</color> and his skill damage dealt proportional to his <color=white>VF Gauge</color> increase when he uses <color=white>Crescent Viper(Q)</color> and  <color=white>Dry Bite(E)</color>. When his <color=white>VF Gauge</color> is full he enters a <color=white>VF Overflow</color> state for a period of time and his increased damage is applied to the max. \n\n<color=white>VF Overflow:</color> EchionŌĆÖs movement speed is increased and he periodically deals true damage to enemies nearby, activating <color=white>Envenomization(R)</color> and resetting the cooldown of  <color=white>Dry Bite(E)</color>.\nWhile VF Overflow is active, killing an enemy increases the duration of Overflow and resets the cooldown of  <color=white>Dry Bite(E)</color>.\n\n<color=white>Exuviation:</color> When Overflow ends, he enters Exuviation for a period of time, reducing his attack range and disabling <color=white>VF Gauge</color> increase.\n\n<color=white>Envenomization(R):</color> Echion fires unstable VF in a targeted direction, dealing skill damage. The duration of Overflow increases when he deals damage to enemy characters with <color=white>Envenomization</color>. Dealing damage with basic attacks reduces the cooldown of <color=white>Envenomization</color>.\n\n<i>EnvenomizationŌĆÖs effects change based on currently used weapon.</i>\n<i>Fixed Cooldown: This skill is not affected by Cooldown Reduction.</i>

CharacterPlayTip/69ŌöāLeni loves to play pranks on everyone, taunting her enemies and helping her allies with her toys.
CharacterPlayTip/70ŌöāTsubame is a rigorously trained combat agent. She carves marks on her targets with her kunai and cuts through them to deal great damage.
CharacterPlayTip/71ŌöāKenneth unleashes his supressed rage in flames, burning everything that stands in his way and dealing powerful damage.
CharacterPlayTip/72ŌöāKatja uses her drone to locate enemies and target them from a long distance.
CharacterPlayTip/73ŌöāCharlotte is a kind-hearted character that can use her light to support her allies and neutralize damage.
CharacterPlayTip/74ŌöāDarko will do whatever it takes to get what he wants, including controlling and robbing his enemies until they give in. 
CharacterPlayTip/75ŌöāLenore's music is inspired by the echoes of pain and screams, putting up performances so powerful they can make her audience insane.
CharacterPlayTip/76ŌöāGarnet has a strong obsession with pain and actively enjoys taking and dealing damage on the front lines. \nShe traps both herself and her target in an Iron Maiden, inflicting eternal torment.
CharacterPlayTip/77ŌöāYumin, the Taoist, moves effortlessly through enemies like the wind, harnessing his growing power on Wind Zones to control the flow of battle.
CharacterPlayTip/78ŌöāHisui, a member of Nichirinkai, is on the hunt for someone who betrayed the organization. Passionate about collecting swords, she channels their memories and powers, unleashing a wide array of techniques to dominate the battlefield.
CharacterPlayTip/79ŌöāJustyna is an outgoing girl who dreams of becoming a hero of justice. She delivers continuous attacks with relentless precision, unleashing powerful energy blasts capable of devastating entire enemy zones.
CharacterPlayTip/80ŌöāIstvan grows stronger, unlocking an alternate version of himself that produces different results from his usual actions. He constantly explores various possibilities in each combat, running experiments over and over to achieve better outcomes.
CharacterPlayTip/81ŌöāNiaH loves video games. She controls reality like a video game and she fights her enemies by limiting their movement, engaging in amazing fights. 
CharacterPlayTip/82ŌöāXuelin is a humble country girl trained in the art of psychic swordsmanship. She attacks enemies by throwing her energy blades at them or uses them to defend herself. She can also dive into enemy lines in an instant to turn the tide of battle.
CharacterPlayTip/83ŌöāHenry manipulates time to block enemies and create optimal conditions for his allies.
CharacterPlayTip/84ŌöāFueled by her deep hatred toward VF Awakeners, Blair uses the XMS-5 to enhance her physical abilities and gain the power to detect them. She skillfully connects her abilities to power over her oponents in close combat.
CharacterPlayTip/85ŌöāMirka is a test subject that initiates fights with CC and creates opportunities by filling up her Impulse Gauge during combat.
CharacterPlayTip/86ŌöāBorn with innate wild instincts and animalistic senses, Fenrir is a test subject who utilizes his distinct physical features, such as his tail and claws, to engage in combat.\nPossessing a fierce tenacity for life, Fenrir refuses to yield even to death and fights back until the very end.
CharacterPlayTip/87ŌöāCoraline is a test subject who zones enemies at long range by reflecting her projectiles off mirrors.
MasteryType/Glove/DescŌöāGloves have a short range, but you can extend that range when using their weapon skill.
MasteryType/Tonfa/DescŌöāTonfas are weapons that are also capable of providing good defense.
MasteryType/Hammer/DescŌöāHammers have a very low attack speed, but they can slow enemies with a single strong blow.
MasteryType/Bat/DescŌöāBats are flexible and balanced weapons that can deal with diverse enemies easily.
MasteryType/Whip/DescŌöāWhips have a short attack range, but allow you to pull your foes closer to you with the relatively long ranged weapon skill.
MasteryType/HighAngleFire/DescŌöāThrow weapons often have high attack power. The amount of stacks that you can hold, as well as how many are made, vary between weapons, so make sure to keep track of how many you have and can craft.
MasteryType/DirectFire/DescŌöāShurikens have a large variety of weapons and are very versatile. The amount of stacks that you can hold, as well as how many are made, vary between weapons, so make sure to keep track of how many you have and can craft.
MasteryType/Bow/DescŌöāBows are long-ranged weapons that get stronger the more mastery you obtain.They are especially effective against wild animals.
MasteryType/CrossBow/DescŌöāCrossbows users rely on their capacity to stun enemies and deal constant damage to them.. 
MasteryType/Pistol/DescŌöāPistols let you use skills more often.These weapons are specialized in agility and stylish mobility. 
MasteryType/AssaultRifle/DescŌöāAssault rifles are long-ranged weapons that fire in bursts and specialize in rapid and constant attacks.  
MasteryType/SniperRifle/DescŌöāSniper rifles have high Attack Power and the longest attack range. They cover more distance than range of vision, so be sure to carefully select a position and secure a good range of vision.
MasteryType/Axe/DescŌöāAxes might slow you down, but they hurt. Weapons that are fast to craft give you a time advantage in battle.
MasteryType/OneHandSword/DescŌöāDaggers are specialized for surprise attacks. They excel in quick, short range battles.
MasteryType/TwoHandSword/DescŌöāTwo-handed swords are well-balanced weapons. The more time you spend mastering them, the more you will appreciate them.
MasteryType/Polearm/DescŌöāPolearms have the most balance of destructive power and long range - especially when dealing with many enemies at the same time. 
MasteryType/DualSword/DescŌöāDual Swords specialize in unleashing two stanzas of beautiful, consecutive attacks.
MasteryType/Spear/DescŌöāSpears may look like they unleash mid-ranged attacks with high destruction power. However, they are weak against enemies who get too close. It is important to control your distance with spears.
MasteryType/Nunchaku/DescŌöāNunchaku are hard to learn, but can be a versatile weapon with creative uses if mastered.
MasteryType/Rapier/DescŌöāRapiers are capable of flashy movements that dodge enemy attacks, making their own attacks a success. 
MasteryType/Guitar/DescŌöāGuitars are long-ranged weapons that use sound waves. These weapons are very versatile as your gameplay and strategy can change completely depending on the number of Evolution points applied to her skills.
MasteryType/Camera/DescŌöāCameras are long-range weapons without any projectiles. They can also be used to blind enemies, which causing them to miss from the bright flash.
MasteryType/Arcana/DescŌöāArcana are ranged weapons capable of manipulating pure VF energy. Arcana users take down their enemies with endless skillcasting.
MasteryType/VFArm/DescŌöāVF Prosthetics are melee weapons that utilize nanomachines to control VF. Wielders can adjust the flow of VF to deal severe damage at once.

WeaponType/Desc/TonfaŌöāTonfa is a melee weapon with high viability.
WeaponType/Desc/BatŌöāBat is a melee weapon that can handle enemies with ease.
WeaponType/Desc/WhipŌöāWhip is a melee weapon specialized in controlling ranged battles.
WeaponType/Desc/HighAngleFireŌöāThrow is a ranged weapon that is perfect for blocking vision during long-range battles.
WeaponType/Desc/DirectFireŌöāShuriken is a ranged weapon that deals heavy damage for both skills and basic attacks.
WeaponType/Desc/BowŌöāBow is a ranged weapon that has the potential for high growth.
WeaponType/Desc/CrossBowŌöāCrossbow is a ranged weapon that holds approaching enemies back and controls ranged fighting.
WeaponType/Desc/PistolŌöāPistol is a ranged weapon that offers stylish/flashy combat through quick attack speed.
WeaponType/Desc/AssaultRifleŌöāAssault Rifle is a ranged weapon that is proficient in continuous combat through fast attack speed.
WeaponType/Desc/SniperRifleŌöāSniper Rifle is a ranged weapon best used for long ranges and strong single-shot attacks.
WeaponType/Desc/HammerŌöāHammer is a melee weapon that is slow to attack but offers powerful hits.
WeaponType/Desc/AxeŌöāAxe is a melee weapon built to last in combat.
WeaponType/Desc/OneHandSwordŌöāDagger is a melee weapon best for surprise attacks and instant, close-range battle.
WeaponType/Desc/TwoHandSwordŌöāTwo-handed Sword is a melee weapon that is well-balanced in all stats.
WeaponType/Desc/DualSwordŌöāDuals Swords are melee weapons capable of stylish combat.
WeaponType/Desc/SpearŌöāSpear is a melee weapon made for mid-range battle.
WeaponType/Desc/NunchakuŌöāNunchaku is a melee weapon that is versatile in different kinds of battle.
WeaponType/Desc/RapierŌöāRapier is a melee weapon that allows you to quickly rush your enemy.
WeaponType/Desc/GuitarŌöāGuitar is a ranged weapon optimitzed to support your allies.
WeaponType/Desc/CameraŌöāCamera is a ranged weapon that can block enemy vision range.
WeaponType/Desc/ArcanaŌöāArcana is a ranged weapon that uses a combination of magic to fight.
WeaponType/Desc/VFArmŌöāVF Prosthetics is a melee weapon that can be upgraded without combining different weapons.

gameTip_Title_10010_1ŌöāCrafting
gameTip_Desc_10010_1ŌöāCrafting is a must to survive! Your most necessary items start from the left for better accessibility.

gameTip_Title_10030_1ŌöāWild Animals
gameTip_Desc_10030_1ŌöāHunt Lumia Island's wild animals to get Credits and items.\n\nChickens, bats, bears, and wolves are just a few of the wild animals on Lumia Island. gameTip_Title_10031_1ŌöāWild Animals - Mutants
gameTip_Desc_10031_1ŌöāHave you noticed that some of Lumia Island's Wild Animals look a bit different?\n\nThese enhanced Wild Animals are called "mutants." Mutants are much stronger than regular Wild Animals, and you can get better rewards and more Credits for killing them.\n\nMutants appear after a certain number of kills of each Wild Animal, or they appear later in game in powerful packs that roam with regular Wild Animals.\n\n
gameTip_Title_10032_1ŌöāBoss Monsters
gameTip_Desc_10032_1ŌöāDo you know the powerful boss monsters who appear on Lumia Island: Wickeline, Alpha, and Omega?\n\nThey appear at different times, but you can check the times by clicking the <color=#8FDBF8>[TAB Hotkey]</color> and where they'll appear by clicking the <color=#8FDBF8>[M Hotkey]</color>. You can also see the location on the Minimap!\n\nYou get special rewards (buffs, special materials, etc.) if you take out the boss monsters. Be warned though: if you're not prepared, you can be defeated or ambushed by other Test Subjects. Always be on your guard when taking on a boss monster!
gameTip_Title_10040_1ŌöāSpecial Materials
gameTip_Desc_10040_1ŌöāSpecial materials are needed to make powerful items!\n\nAs the materials are special, the way to get them is just as special.\n\nYou can get these materials at certains times in areas of the map, such as the Tree of Life or Meteorite, or can be purchased from Kiosks with Credits, hunting boss monsters, or by opening Air Supply Boxes.

gameTip_Title_10060_1ŌöāInformation Overlay

gameTip_Title_10070_1ŌöāSecurity Console
gameTip_Desc_10070_1ŌöāActivate the security console in each area of Lumia Island to gain important sight on the enemies' location. Use this information to gain the upper hand!\n\nBy activating a security console in an area, you'll be able to see CCTVs that shows the area around the camera. Also, activating a security console that is not occupied by an ally will grant you a small amount of Credits,\n\nOn the other hand, if the CCTV is moving closer to you and blinking red, that means someone is watching you instead! Run for it!
gameTip_Title_10071_1ŌöāHyperloop & VLS
gameTip_Desc_10071_1ŌöāBig changes are coming to the Hyperloop system.\nStarting on Day 3, the new VLS transportation devices will be available in Safe Zones, while Hyperloops will be dynamically activated in Restricted Areas.\nUnlike Hyperloops, the VLS allows movement by "gliding" within a certain radius.
gameTip_Title_10071_2ŌöāHyperloop
gameTip_Desc_10071_2ŌöāDid someone say, FAST TRAVEL?\n\nIt may look like a broken down phone booth, but this booth allows you to travel anywhere on Lumia Island thanks to Hyperloop technology. You can also tap on a <color=#8FDBF8>teammate's picture</color> to be teleported directly to them!\n\n*Hyperloop Usage Precautions*\nYou may accidentally run into enemies after teleporting. Be careful!
gameTip_Title_10071_3ŌöāVLS
gameTip_Desc_10071_3ŌöāUnlike Hyperloops, the VLS isn't designed for long-distance travel but enables synchronized gliding with allies.\nWhile you're gliding, the ground becomes visible below a certain altitude, making it excellent for scouting.\nLanding after gliding triggers a brief area-wide stun, making it useful for initiating.\nOnly the Host can control the gliding, with allies relying on the Host for synchronized movement. At a certain altitude, allies can break away from the Host and move freely.
gameTip_Title_10072_1ŌöāLaunch Pad
gameTip_Desc_10072_1ŌöāThe Launch Pad allows you to easily and quickly move to distant areas!\nThey can be crucial in confrontations with enemies, so practice using them strategically!
gameTip_Title_10090_1ŌöāQuick Communication
gameTip_Desc_10090_1ŌöāYou can ping anyone by clicking ALT + the left mouse button.\n\nEven if there's no time to chat, use the ping function to communicate in a variety of ways. Send an exclamation point to allies to alert them to "danger ahead!"\n\nIf you need a bit more in-depth communication, click CTRL and drag the left mouse button for the <color=#8FDBF8>quick chatting function</color>. Click CTRL + the left mouse button to <color=#8FDBF8>set goals with your team</color>.
gameTip_Title_10100_1ŌöāDetonation Timer
gameTip_Desc_10100_1ŌöāIf you're in a Restricted Area marked in red, you better get out of there, stat! If not, you'll die as soon as the detonation timer goes off.\n\nDetonation timers are placed in Restricted Areas and activate with terrifying effects. When the timer reaches 0, you can kiss your life goodbye.\n\nYou can restore the detonation timer by killing other test subjects, but it's better for you not to be there at all.\n\n"Soon the Test Subject will explode!"
gameTip_Title_10110_1ŌöāTemporary Safe Zone
gameTip_Desc_10110_1ŌöāWhen the last safe zone is designated as a Restricted Area, two temporary safe zones are created if <color=#8FDBF8>3 or more survivors</color> are still alive.\n\nThe detonation timer won't tick down if your team is inside the safe zone. On the other hand, if 2 or more teams are in the temporary safe zone, or if they leave the temporary safe zone, the detonation timer begins ticking again.\n\nIt's a piece of cake. Keep your team in the temporary safe zone, and drive out any enemies to stay alive!
gameTip_Title_10120_1ŌöāFinal Safe Zone
gameTip_Desc_10120_1ŌöāThe final safe zone is a place to determine the ultimate winner.\n\nThe final safe zone forms in a circular area around the Chronosphere located in the last restricted area. Outside of this safe zone, the explosion timer will activate.\n\nAfter a certain period of time, the safe zones will gradually begin to disappear. Eventually, all safe zones will disappear, and the explosion timer will activate across the entire map. This is your time to defeat the enemy and claim first place!
gameTip_Title_10130_1ŌöāAuto Revive
gameTip_Desc_10130_1ŌöāDied? Don't give up just yet!\n\nIf your teammates are still alive, you will auto-revive until Night 2. An enemy guarding your dead body? No problem - you can choose the spot to respawn. A place close to your team should be safe... right?
gameTip_Title_10140_1ŌöāDowned / Slain
gameTip_Desc_10140_1ŌöāTest subjects on Lumia Island become downed when they are first slain. When down, you can only move or discard items, and your detonation timer and HP drop even if you stand still.\n\nIf you die again while down, your dead body will be left behind. You can grab items left from dead test subjects. If you hunt a test subject that has a Legendary item, you can earn a large amount of Credits through bounty items.
gameTip_Title_10140_2ŌöāDowned Skill
gameTip_Desc_10140_2ŌöāDon't give up when you're downed!\nThere are some special skills that can only be used while downed!\n\nThere are a total of 3 Downed Skills:\n1. <color=#8FDBF8>Obstinacy</color>, which temporarily increases movement speed while downed. \n2. <color=#8FDBF8>Fission Blast</color>, which makes test subjects to self-destruct.\n3. <color=#8FDBF8>AED</color>, which allows test subjects to self-revive after a certain amount of time.\n\nCreate a more diverse gameplay by strategically using Downed Skills!
gameTip_Title_10150_1ŌöāAir Supply Box
gameTip_Desc_10150_1ŌöāAre you out of food? Or maybe you didn't craft the equipment you need? If so, look for the Air Supply Box on the map!\n\nYou can get food from a food box, more powerful materials from an Air Supply Box, and Legendary equiment in a legendary-level box. Open up the box and hope what you need is inside!\n\nDon't miss out when warnings of where and when the reward boxes will drop appear on the minimap.
gameTip_Title_10160_1ŌöāLevel / Skill
gameTip_Desc_10160_1ŌöāUse the points you gain to level up and use stronger skills!\n\nWhen you obtain a certain level of mastery, the test subject's "level" increases. As level goes up, so do stats. Gain skill points to use even stronger skills\n\nLevel up and become as strong as you can!
gameTip_Title_10170_1ŌöāAugments
gameTip_Desc_10170_1ŌöāYou can add new skills and buffs to your test subjects by selecting Augments.\n\nAugments allow more variety and options. For example, using Fortification Augments will increase Defense and reduce Damage. If you use Support Augments, you may be able to help your teammates and lead them to victory!\n\nDon't know which Augments are better suited for your test subjects? Check them out in the <color=#8FDBF8>[Loadout ŌåÆ Augments Menu]</color>!
gameTip_Title_10220_1ŌöāTactical Skills
gameTip_Desc_10220_1ŌöāTactical skills are crucial in the game, so it's important to choose and use them wisely based on your team, enemy selection, and your own test subject's style. Have fun strategizing and enjoying a variety of tactical skills!\n\n*You can <color=#8FDBF8>enhance</color> tactical skills by spending Credits. Level 2 upgrades require <color=#8FDBF8>special materials</color>.
gameTip_Title_10230_1ŌöāOperator
gameTip_Desc_10230_1ŌöāNot sure what to do?\n\nJust follow an operator who can guide you.\nThey'll show you the right path for your situation.\n\nYou can turn them on and off in the [Settings], and you can choose the kind of guidance you want in the [Custom Game] presets.
gameTip_Title_10240_1ŌöāCredit Revival
gameTip_Desc_10240_1ŌöāDon't give up just because you're dead!\n\nTeammates can spend a certain amount of credits to revive a dead teammate at Kiosk.\nHowever, you can't choose the respawn point, so it's best to let your teammates know in advance.
gameTip_Title_10250_1ŌöāCampfire
gameTip_Title_10260_1ŌöāV-Pad
gameTip_Desc_10260_1ŌöāMastery, tactical skills, item management... Too much to think about, right?\nDon't worry, just press V! \n\nWith the new VPad, you'll be able to see all the information you need to progress in the game at a glance.\nVPad is here to save you the trouble!
gameTip_Title_10270_1ŌöāEscape
gameTip_Desc_10270_1ŌöāAnother way to end the game is by obtaining the escape item and initiating a vote to leave Lumia Island via submarine.\n\nIf you successfully escape, you'll earn a <color=#8FDBF8>Fixed Amount of Rank Points</color>!
gameTip_Title_10270_2ŌöāEscape Conditions
gameTip_Desc_10270_2ŌöāYou can escape when the following <color=#8FDBF8>5 conditions</color> are met using the <color=#8FDBF8>Loop Card Key - Red</color>:\n\n1. At least 1 of your teammates has died\n2. At least 3 teams are still alive\n3. It's Day 4 onwards\n4. No team has successfully escaped\n5. You have the escape item and have voted to escape
gameTip_Title_10270_3ŌöāEscape Item
gameTip_Desc_10270_3ŌöāYou need a <color=#8FDBF8>Loop Card Key - Red</color> to escape the experiment.\n\nThe Loop Card Key - Red can be purchased with credits at Kiosks, and are limited to a <color=#8FDBF8>maximum of 3 teams</color>.
gameTip_Title_10270_4ŌöāEscape Voting
gameTip_Desc_10270_4ŌöāYour team has to vote in order to escape.\nOnce the team meets the escape conditions and purchases a Loop Card Key - Red from a Kiosk, a vote is conducted.\n\nThere are two rounds of voting, each lasting 30 seconds. You can only escape if the majority of the team agrees to escape. If you don't participate, your vote counts as in favor.\n\nThe second round of voting is automatically carried a certain amount of time later.
gameTip_Title_10270_5ŌöāHow to Escape
gameTip_Desc_10270_5ŌöāYou can escape a moment after using the <color=#8FDBF8>Loop Card Key - Red</color> you purchased from the Kiosk.
gameTip_Title_10280_1ŌöāCrafting Menu's Quick Slot
gameTip_Desc_10280_1ŌöāLow on health with no food, or trying to track down a fleeing opponent without a drone?\nWe've all been there.\n\nWhen that happens, just hit the V key to open your Crafting Quick Slot!\n\nYou can order all sorts of items straight to your inventory from this screen.\nJust keep in mind that placing an order costs a few Credits.
gameTip_Title_10280_2ŌöāGadget Vault
gameTip_Desc_10280_2ŌöāYou can find a varietu of gadgets that can help you thrive in Lumia Island.\n\nPress G to see your options!
gameTip_Title_10280_3ŌöāUse Gadget Vault
gameTip_Desc_10280_3ŌöāOn Lumia Island, you gain a small amount of Gadget Points each time a Day passes.\n\nAccumulate Gadget Points to unlock various useful items such as the HunterŌĆÖs Pot, Kiosk Remote, Mobile VLS, and even ORBs, and use them at the right moment!
gameTip_Title_11000_1ŌöāWhat is Cobalt?
gameTip_Desc_11000_1ŌöāA mode where you can enjoy a combat-oriented 4vs4 battle.\nDeduct the opposing team's life points through battle and capture to secure victory!
gameTip_Title_11000_2ŌöāTest Subject Selection
gameTip_Desc_11000_2ŌöāSelect <color=#8FDBF8>your character</color> at the start of the character selection phase.\n\nYour opponents may also have the same characters as you or your allies.\n\nYou cannot use characters that you don't own or that are not in the free rotation.
gameTip_Title_11000_3ŌöāVictory Conditions
gameTip_Desc_11000_3ŌöāThe first team to reduce 45 life points from the other team wins!\nLife points can be reduced by:\n\n1. Eliminating an enemy test subject: -1 point\n2. Taking over the Power Beacon for 40 seconds: -1 point\n3, Defeating Gamma: -5 points
gameTip_Title_11000_4ŌöāPhase 4
gameTip_Desc_11000_4ŌöāIf a winner isn't decided within the time frame, the <color=#8FDBF8>sudden death phase</color> kicks in to settle the final result.\nEach team's Life Points are reset to 1.
gameTip_Title_11000_5ŌöāItems
gameTip_Desc_11000_5ŌöāAt the start of each game, there is an equipment draft with Epic items. \n\nRecommended items based on your character and weapon type are displayed, but you're free to choose others.\n\nYou can purchase items through the Synthesizer (B)'s Extra Equipment Draft.
gameTip_Title_11000_6ŌöāInfusions
gameTip_Desc_11000_6ŌöāIn each phase (1, 2, 3), you can select 1 of 3 infusions.\nIf you donŌĆÖt like the options, you can refresh each infusion once for a different choice.\nOnce you select an infusion, you <color=#8FDBF8>cannot exchange it</color> for another!
gameTip_Title_11000_7ŌöāSynthesizer
gameTip_Desc_11000_7ŌöāYou can use it when you're in the Control Room or by pressing the B key upon being eliminated.\n\nYou can cannot use the Synthesizer if you leave your team's base.
gameTip_Title_11000_8ŌöāOmega/Gamma
gameTip_Desc_11000_8Ōöā<color=#8FDBF8>Omega</color> appears in Phase 2 and can grant a large amount of credits when defeated.\n\n<color=#8FDBF8>Gamma</color> appears in Phase 3 and can cut the opponent team's life points when defeated.\n\nThese are very strong monsters, so be careful!
gameTip_Title_11000_9ŌöāPower Beacon
gameTip_Desc_11000_9ŌöāCapture the <color=#8FDBF8>3 Power Beacons</color> located in the center of the map.\nCaptured Power Beacons deduct the opponent team's life points after a certain amount of time.  \nIn Phase 3, all Power Beacons except the central one are deactivated. Life Points will be immediately deducted depending on whether they were captured or not.
gameTip_Title_11000_10ŌöāCredits
gameTip_Desc_11000_10ŌöāYou can earn credits through:\n\n1. Capturing Power Beacons\n2. Natural Gain (Periodically throughout the match)\n3. Kills/Assists\n4. Hunting Boss Monsters
gameTip_Title_11000_11ŌöāCobalt Tactical Skills
gameTip_Desc_11000_11ŌöāYou can use special tactical skills that are different from the ones on Lumia Island.\n\nAt the start of each game, after the Epic equipment draft, you can select 1 of 6 tactical skills. \n2 are fixed options and 4 are randomly drafted to you. Pick your favorite one!
gameTip_Title_11000_12ŌöāAdditional Supply Boxes
gameTip_Desc_11000_12Ōöā<color=#8FDBF8>Mystery Supply Box items</color> appear in Phase 3.\n\nYou can get very strong equipment from the Control Room's Supply Box in Phase 3 depending on your score difference.
gameTip_Title_10160_2ŌöāMastery
gameTip_Desc_10160_2Ōöā<color=#8FDBF8>Mastery</color> is divided into Defense, Hunting, Crafting, Movement, Search, and Weapons.\nYou gain Mastery when you meet the requirements for each of the above, and you level up when you accumulate a certain number of Masteries.\nYou can learn more about the requirements on the <color=#8FDBF8>Character Info Section</color>.
gameTip_Title_10070_3ŌöāLaunch Pad
gameTip_Desc_10070_3ŌöāThe Launch Pad allows you to easily and quickly move to distant areas!\nThey can be crucial in confrontations with enemies, so practice using them strategically!
gameTip_Title_10401_1ŌöāIntroducing Weather Variables
gameTip_Desc_10401_1ŌöāLumia Island's weather is influenced by 1 main and 1 sub weather, with different effects being applied based on the corresponding weather conditions.\nYou can check which weather variables you've been assigned through the cinematic intro after matching or in-game.
gameTip_Title_10402_1ŌöāMain Weather
gameTip_Desc_10402_1ŌöāThe main weather types are Cloudy, Clear, Rainy and Sandstorm.\n\nCloudy: Lumia Island's default weather with no effects applied.\n\nClear: An optimal environment. This weather facilitates food crafting and visibility.\n\nRainy: Perfect for hiding. The number of brushes in the island is increased and hearing range is reduced.\n\nSandstorm: A very restrictive environment. Test subjects gain tenacity and whirlwinds are created.
gameTip_Title_10402_2ŌöāMain Weather - Clear
gameTip_Desc_10402_2ŌöāOverall there are less brushes around the island and your vision range is increased.\nYour recovery from crafted consumables is also increased.\nYou have higher chances to get Rare Materials from collecting consumables.
gameTip_Title_10402_3ŌöāMain Weather - Rainy
gameTip_Desc_10402_3ŌöāOverall there are more brushes around the island.\nYour hearing range decreases and wild animals' attack power is reduced. 
gameTip_Title_10402_4ŌöāMain Weather - Sandstorm
gameTip_Desc_10402_4ŌöāTest subjects that withstand the harsh sandtorm gain Tenacity.\nA whirlwind is created, making targets hit by it untargetable. Targets caught in the whirlwind are able to move over walls after a certain time. 
gameTip_Title_10403_1ŌöāSub Weather
gameTip_Desc_10403_1ŌöāThe sub weather types are Windless, Purple Fog, Strong Winds and Lightning.\n\nWindless: Lumia Island's default weather with no effects applied.\n\nPurple Fog: Mutant spawn rates increase, and all characters deal increased damage to wild animals. \n\nStrong Winds: Perfect for moving faster. Your basic movement speed increases and strong wind zones increase your movement speed in one direction.\n\nLightning: A threatening environment. Lightning strikes occur randomly outdoors, except in restricted areas.
gameTip_Title_10403_2ŌöāSub Weather - Purple Fog
gameTip_Desc_10403_2ŌöāDuring certain phases, Fog Zones are created. These zones have the following effects:\nAll wild animals within the Fog zones are eliminated.\nFog Nests spawn at random locations within the Fog zones.\nApproaching a Fog Nest spawns an aggressive herd of mutant monsters.
gameTip_Title_10403_3ŌöāSub Weather - Strong Winds
gameTip_Desc_10403_3ŌöāYour base movement speed is increased.\nStrong Wind zones are created in specific areas, increasing your movement speed in one direction.
gameTip_Title_10403_4ŌöāSub Weather - Lightning
gameTip_Desc_10403_4ŌöāLightning strikes happen all around the island except in Restricted Areas.\nThe frequency of lightning strikes increases over the course of the experiment.
gameTip_Title_10404_1ŌöāIntroducing Eco Objectives
gameTip_Desc_10404_1ŌöāThere are 5 Eco Objectives and each of them apply different effects. These are: Glowing Flower, White Lily, Healing Spray, Sandy Mushroom and Bomb Fruit.
gameTip_Title_10404_2ŌöāEco Objective - Glowing Flower
gameTip_Desc_10404_2ŌöāThe flower emits a fan-shaped wave in the direction of the attack.\nThe fan-shaped wave travels a certain distance, granting vision and making you visible to your enemy test subjects within range for a certain period of time.
gameTip_Title_10404_3ŌöāEco Objective - White Lily
gameTip_Desc_10404_3ŌöāTest subjects within range are knocked back.\nAirborne test subjects can go over walls.
gameTip_Title_10404_4ŌöāEco Objective - Healing Spray
gameTip_Desc_10404_4ŌöāWhen the objective is under attack it sprays a mist that heals tests subjects within a certain range.\nHealing is applied to both allies and enemies.
gameTip_Title_10404_5ŌöāEco Objective - Sandy Mushroom
gameTip_Desc_10404_5ŌöāThis objective releases cloud of sand, granting invisibility within range for a certain period of time.
gameTip_Title_10404_6ŌöāEco Objective - Bomb Fruit
gameTip_Desc_10404_6ŌöāFruits are released in a fan shape in the direction of the attack, dealing damage.\nFruit damage does not affect allies and is proportional to max hp.\nThe fruits also deal additional damage to Wild Animals.
gameTip_Title_10501_1ŌöāGadget Introduction
gameTip_Desc_10501_1ŌöāGadgets are usable items that provide useful effects during experiments.\nYou can obtain gadgets through various means such as mutant wild animals or air supplies, and each player can only hold one type of gadget.
gameTip_Title_10502_1ŌöāKiosk Remote
gameTip_Desc_10502_1ŌöāHave you ever been unable to use Credits because the area where the Kiosk was located was occupied?\nNow, with the 'Kiosk Remote' gadget obtained from mutant wild animals, you can summon a Mobile Kiosk drone from any location and use your credits.
gameTip_Title_10503_1ŌöāFlare Guns
gameTip_Desc_10503_1ŌöāThese flare guns embody the saying, "A crisis is an opportunity."\nCollect one of the two types of flare guns, Flare Gun B and Flare Gun R, from Epic Air Supply boxes and turn crises into opportunities.
gameTip_Title_10503_2ŌöāFlare Gun B
gameTip_Desc_10503_2ŌöāUsing the Flare Gun B allows you to select a specific point within a wide area to call a Mythic Air Supply box.\nThe location is revealed to all test subjects, but it can turn a crisis into an opportunity as you get a chance to acquire powerful mythic items.
gameTip_Title_10503_3ŌöāFlare Gun R
gameTip_Desc_10503_3ŌöāUse the Flare Gun R to become the spawn point of the air supply.\n(Upon death, the supply box will arrive to the location of your corpse)\nAlthough the location is revealed to all test subjects within a certain range, overcoming this crisis can lead to obtaining powerful mythic items.
gameTip_Title_10504_1ŌöāMobile VLS
gameTip_Desc_10504_1ŌöāThe Mobile VLS provides the same functionality as the standard VLS but is a single-use gadget that enables synchronized gliding with allies.\nWhile you're gliding, the ground becomes visible below a certain altitude, making it excellent for scouting.\nLanding after gliding triggers a brief area-wide stun, making it useful for initiating.\nOnly the Host can control the gliding, with allies relying on the Host for synchronized movement. At a certain altitude, allies can break away from the Host and move freely.
gameTip_Title_10511_1ŌöāGuide Robot - Lumi
gameTip_Desc_10511_1ŌöāLumi is a cute guide robot that roams around Lumia Island, selling special items.\nOnce it accumulates a certain amount of credits, you can steal them from Lumi.
gameTip_Title_10512_1ŌöāGuide Robot - Special Shop
gameTip_Desc_10512_1ŌöāLumi will stop and greet you when you get close to it during the game. Right-click on it to interact with it and purchase special items not available at Kiosks at a low price. Once you buy an item, the guide robot will dash away at high speed. If you don't purchase anything, it will take a short break, temporarily stop selling items and move around slowly.
gameTip_Title_10513_1ŌöāGuide Robot: Credits Charged
gameTip_Desc_10513_1ŌöāSpend Credits at the Kiosks, with the Guide Robot, or using Remote Drones to help the Guide Robot accumulate enough Credits to enter a "Credits Charged" state.\nApproach the Guide Robot in this state and right-click to channel for a short time. The Guide Robot will then enter a 'Alert Mode', shooting energy projectiles at nearby test subjects as it flees.\nAttack the fleeing Guide Robot and deal enough damage to make it drop Credits on the ground. Collecting these Credits will reward you with an amount of Credits proportional to your character's level.
gameTip_Title_10514_1ŌöāGuide Robot - Detailed Explanation
gameTip_Desc_10514_1ŌöāThe Guide Robot's revival function is available until Restricted Areas become active after Day 5. If you lose a teammate during late game, look for the Guide Robot for a turnaround.\nYou can always check the Guide Robot's location on the Expanded Map/Minimap. Since the Guide Robot dislikes Restricted Areas, it moves at five times the normal speed within them.
gameTip_Title_10521_1ŌöāWhat are Cubes?
gameTip_Desc_10521_1ŌöāCubes are objectives that grant special effects to the character who collects them, depending on their type.\nThe type of cube is determined one minute before it spawns, and you can obtain it by directly touching the cube once it spawns.
gameTip_Title_10522_1ŌöāSanguine Cube
gameTip_Desc_10522_1ŌöāThe red Sanguine Cube grants the character who collects it a powerful ability to deal continuous damage over time.
gameTip_Title_10523_1ŌöāGalaxy Cube
gameTip_Desc_10523_1ŌöāThe purple Galaxy Cube grants the character who collects it reduced cooldowns, an increased max cooldown reduction and a skill amp increase, as well as partial recovery of lost SP periodically and additional damage based on Skill Amplification.
gameTip_Title_10524_1ŌöāProsperity Cube
gameTip_Desc_10524_1ŌöāThe golden Prosperity Cube grants the character who collects it Credits proportional to their level shortly after.
gameTip_Title_10525_1ŌöāEssence Cube
gameTip_Desc_10525_1ŌöāThe green Essence Cube grants the character who collects it the ability to recover health proportional to their max HP for a short time when their HP drops below a certain amount and they take damage.
gameTip_Title_10526_1ŌöāZephyr Cube
gameTip_Desc_10526_1ŌöāThe sky-blue Zephyr Cube grants the character who acquires it a burst of movement speed for a short time after collecting it, significantly increasing their movement speed, out-of-combat movement speed and attack speed for the duration of the buff. During combat, movement speed is increased.
gameTip_Title_10621_1ŌöāWhat is a Secret Lab?
gameTip_Desc_10621_1ŌöāA Secret Lab is a hidden location within Lumia Island where you can obtain special rewards without other teams getting on your way.\nYou can only enter the lab using the Mobile Loop that is activated with a Loop Key Card and you can return to Lumia Island using the Loop inside the lab.
gameTip_Title_10622_1ŌöāLoop Card Keys
gameTip_Desc_10622_1ŌöāThe Loop Key Cards grant access to the Secret Lab. These can be obtained from Wild Animals.
gameTip_Title_10623_1ŌöāMobile Loop
gameTip_Desc_10623_1ŌöāUse the Loop Key Card to activate a portable loop that grants access to a Secret Lab.\n\nThe portable loop can only be accessed at designated times during the game, and you can only access one Secret Lab per game.\n\nIf all labs are full, you've already visited a lab, or the entry time has passed, the loop turns red and deactivates.
gameTip_Title_10624_1ŌöāAbout the Secret Lab
gameTip_Desc_10624_1ŌöāOnce you arrive at a Secret Lab, activate the door console to earn credits and open the door. \n\nExplore the entire lab, kill drones, open boxes, and more. When the timer at the top ends, the lab becomes a Restricted Area. \nMake sure to exit the lab using the loop before you run out of time.
gameTip_Title_10624_2ŌöāBlue Lab
gameTip_Desc_10624_2ŌöāAs you kill the labŌĆÖs drones and push forward, youŌĆÖll eventually come face-to-face with a powerful hidden presence.\nDefeat it to claim special rewards!
gameTip_Title_10801_1ŌöāIntroducing Rift
gameTip_Desc_10801_1ŌöāThe Rift is a phenomenon caused by abnormal VF energy. It is an objective that teleports nearby test subjects to another space. The type of Rift spawned varies depending on the time of appearance and the number of remaining teams.
gameTip_Title_10802_1ŌöāDimensional Rift
gameTip_Desc_10802_1ŌöāThis Rift spawns mainly during the day. It teleports any test subjects who enter it to the Combat Lab. A Dimensional Rift can be used by only one team.
gameTip_Title_10802_2ŌöāCombat Lab
gameTip_Desc_10802_2ŌöāTest subjects who enter through the Dimensional Rift are teleported to the Combat Lab, where all dead allies are revived. Only two teams can enter the Dimensional Rift and the team that achieves victory earns a powerful reward. Wiped teams in a Combat Lab accesible through a regular Dimensional Rift are revived. However, teams wiped out in a Combat Lab entered through an Enhanced Dimensional Rift (available from Day 3) are eliminated from the experiment.
gameTip_Title_10802_3ŌöāDimensional Rift - Enhanced
gameTip_Desc_10802_3ŌöāFrom Day 3 onward, the Dimensional Rift and Combat Lab are affected by stronger VF energy, altering their appearance and combat mechanics. If your team is wiped out in the Combat Lab during this phase, you are immediately eliminated from the game. Be fully prepared before stepping into this fight for survival.
gameTip_Title_10803_1ŌöāWarp
gameTip_Desc_10803_1ŌöāTest subjects who remain on the Warp for a set period are sucked in and launched into the sky from the Warp exit at the entrance of the Research Center in the Forest. If you want to travel to a specific location, use the VLS instead. If teammates remain on the same Warp, they can hold hands and move together, just like when using the VLS.
gameTip_Title_10300_1ŌöāIntroducing Area Gimmicks
gameTip_Desc_10300_1ŌöāLumia Island features unique area gimmicks in the form of objectives that match each area's concept and offer new strategic effects.
gameTip_Title_10301_1ŌöāPolice Station - Evidence Boxes
gameTip_Desc_10301_1ŌöāFind Evidence Boxes in the Police Station starting from Day 3.\nYou can select one item which belonged to a downed test subject from the evidence box.
gameTip_Title_10302_1ŌöāSchool - Grandfather Clock
gameTip_Desc_10302_1ŌöāGo to School to find a large Grandfather Clock that turns back time.\nOperate it to recover some of your detonation timer and your skill cooldowns (basic, tactical, and weapon).
gameTip_Title_10303_1ŌöāHospital - Medical Support Network
gameTip_Desc_10303_1ŌöāThe Hospital is an area with an enhanced Medical System that aids in test subjects' survival.\nUse the Hospital Kiosk to revive allies at a discounted price.\nKill Hospital Bears to collect First Aid Kits which grant much higher recovery than any common food items.
gameTip_Title_10304_1ŌöāBarge - Cranes & Barrels
gameTip_Desc_10304_1ŌöāUse the dynamic environment of the Barge to your advantage to disrupt enemies.\nA crane in the area periodically rotates and drops barrels onto the ground.\nStriking a dropped barrel rolls it in the direction of the attack. On impact, the rolling barrel creates a zone that continuously slows test subjects.
gameTip_Title_10305_1ŌöāApple Tree
gameTip_Desc_10305_1ŌöāGet additional supplies from the Apple Tree!\nAttack the tree to get Apples ready for consumption.\nYou can even get Golden Apples, which offer higher hp regen, at a lower chance.
gameTip_Title_10050_3ŌöāBox Group Display
gameTip_Desc_10050_3ŌöāDisplays in blue the boxes closest to the starting point for each area.\nBy opening all the boxes in the same group, you can get at least all the target items for that area.\nThis is important information when deciding your Early-game route, so check the boxes with the <color=#8FDBF8>box group indicator</color> first!

Tutorial/Title/2ŌöāPursuit
Tutorial/Title/3ŌöāHunting
Tutorial/Title/4ŌöāPower Up
Tutorial/Title/5ŌöāMastery
Tutorial/Desc/1ŌöāWelcome to Lumia Island.\n\nJackie will teach you the basics of item searching in order to survive on this island.\n\nTo think you'll learn from a Serial Killer! You're in good hands, right?
Tutorial/Desc/2ŌöāLearn about skills and fighting with the Axe weapon. The axe is good to learn with because it's stronger than other weapons in the beginning of the match.\n\nFind enemies and kill them. The axe will become weaker as the game progresses.
Tutorial/Desc/3ŌöāYou can become stronger by killing wild animals.\n\nYou'll play as Aya. She might seem careless, but she's still a police officer handling a gun.
Tutorial/Desc/4ŌöāTry exploring Lumia Island a bit more.\n\nJackie is an intelligent murderer. Try efficiently finding materials so you can craft several targeted items at the same time.
Tutorial/Desc/5ŌöāYour weapon and defense masteries increase as you fight.\n\nMoving around increases your movement mastery and making equipment increases your crafting mastery.\n\nAya might seem like a weak rookie with a puny sniper rifle,\nbut you'll change your mind once she lands a shot on you after increasing her sniper rifle mastery.
