GuideBook/MainTheme/Title/PREFACEŌöāPreface
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/EternalReturnŌöāEternal Return
GuideBook/Page/Content/EternalReturn/1ŌöāEternal Return is a <color=white>Battle Royale</color> game, where test subjects compete to become the final survivor.
GuideBook/Page/Content/EternalReturn/2ŌöāThis guide will take you through the most basic tips you need to know to master the game from start to end.
GuideBook/MainTheme/Title/GROWTHŌöāOpening
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/EpicReadyŌöāCraft
GuideBook/Page/Content/EpicReady/1Ōöā<color=white>(1/3)</color>\n\nUpon arrival, your first assignment is to craft <color=yellow>starting target items</color> in your Saved Plan.\n\nFrom your spawn point, you will notice <color=yellow>boxes</color> that are highlighted in blue. Boxes hold items that are distinct to the area you are in.\n\nLook out for the items marked with a <color=yellow>yellow triangle</color>. These are the items you need to craft the equipment in your Saved Plan.
GuideBook/Page/Content/EpicReadyTwo/1Ōöā<color=white>(2/3)</color>\n\nYou can also acquire items by <color=white>hunting wild animals, collecting natural resources, and calling remote drones</color>.\n\nPress Z to combine these materials until you craft all the <color=white>target items in your Saved Plan</color>.
GuideBook/Page/Content/EpicReadyThree/1Ōöā<color=white>(3/3)</color>\n\nYou can check your starting target items on the <color=white>upper right corner</color>. Once you've finished crafting your your starting target items, they will automatically be updated into your late game equipments.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/TeamUpŌöāTeam Up
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamUp/1ŌöāOnce you've crafted all the target equipment in your Saved Plan, you can use the <color=yellow>Hyperloop</color> to join your teammates.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamUp/2ŌöāClick on your <color=white>teammate's portrait</color> on the upper right corner of the Hyperloop UI to teleport to their location.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamUp/3ŌöāDuring early game, up until <color=yellow>Night 2</color>, you and your teammates can revive after a brief waiting time.\nYou can select the location where you'd like to revive, including your teammate's location by clicking on their <color=white>portrait</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/HuntŌöāHunt
GuideBook/Page/Content/Hunt/1ŌöāStart hunting <color=yellow>wild animals</color> with your teammates.\nUse the security console in each area to track their locations, which will be displayed on your <color=white>Minimap</color>.
GuideBook/Page/Content/Hunt/2ŌöāHunt wild animals to get <color=yellow>Credits</color> and gain <color=yellow>Mastery</color>. \n<color=yellow>Credits</color> are a valuable resource in Lumia Island that can be used to buy special materials or revive your teammates.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/FindFoodŌöāFood
GuideBook/Page/Content/FindFood/1ŌöāYou can recover HP by consuming <color=yellow>Food</color>.\nThis effect will <color=white>persist even in battle</color>, so be sure to stack them in your inventory.
GuideBook/Page/Content/FindFood/2ŌöāYou can obtain raw ingredients from <color=white>wild animals, boxes, or by collecting them in the wild</color>.\nWhile you can consume them to recover HP, they are not very effective.\n\nFind a <color=yellow>Campfire</color> to cook these raw ingredients into more effective food.\n\nYou can also find moderately effective food in <color=yellow>Food Air Supply Boxes</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/TreeOfLifeŌöāTree of Life / Meteorite
GuideBook/Page/Content/TreeOfLife/1Ōöā<color=yellow>On Day 2</color>, two special meterials will spawn on Lumia Island: <color=yellow>Tree of Life</color> and <color=yellow>Meteorite</color>.\n\n<color=yellow>Tree of Life</color> has a fixed spawn point, while <color=yellow>Meteorite</color> collisions will happen at random locations on the island each game.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TreeOfLife/2Ōöā<color=yellow>Special Materials</color> are <color=white>highly valued rare resources</color> in Lumia Island. This means that you must be ready to <color=white>engage in combat</color> with other teams to acquire these materials.\n\nTry not to lose!
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/UpgradeItemsŌöāEquipment Upgrade
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItems/1ŌöāYou need <color=yellow>Special Materials</color> to craft <color=yellow>Legendary items</color>.
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItems/2ŌöāEach teammate may need different <color=yellow>special materials</color>, so be sure to distribute them strategically.
GuideBook/MainTheme/Title/EXPLORATIONŌöāMiddlegame
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/UpgradeItemsTwoŌöāSpecial Materials
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItemsTwo/1Ōöā<color=white>(1/2)</color>\n\nYou can obtain the special materials needed to craft your late-game equipment from the island's main objectives, like <color=white>Tree of Life, Meteorite, Alpha/Omega and Air Supplies</color>
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItemsTwo/2ŌöāYou can also purchase them from <color=yellow>Kiosks</color> that you can find in different areas across the island.
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItemsThree/1Ōöā<color=white>(2/2)</color>\n\n<color=yellow>Main objectives</color> are usually displayed on your <color=white>Minimap</color>. \nYou can also check their spawn times on the Scoreboard by pressing the <color=white>Tab Key</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/RestrictedAreaŌöāRestricted Areas
GuideBook/Page/Content/RestrictedArea/1ŌöāEach Day and Night, a random area on the island will become a <color=red>Restricted Area</color>, marked in <color=red>red</color> on your Expanded Map. Upcoming Restricted Areas are marked in <color=yellow>orange</color> before they're designated as such.
GuideBook/Page/Content/RestrictedArea/2ŌöāEvery second spent at a Restricted Area will consume your <color=red>restriction timer</color>. Once you run out of time, you are instantly <color=red>eliminated</color>.
GuideBook/Page/Content/RestrictedArea/3ŌöāHowever, donŌĆÖt hesitate to use your restriction timer during a <color=white>tough situation</color>. Sometimes, <color=white>escaping into a Restricted Area</color> can be the smart choice for survival. After all, you can restore your timer by <color=white>eliminating enemy characters</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/TransportŌöāTransportation
GuideBook/Page/Content/Transport/1ŌöāThere are various means of transportation on Lumia Island.\n\n<color=yellow>Hyperloops</color> take you to any area you choose or directly to your teammateŌĆÖs location.\n<color=yellow>White Lily</color> lets you jump a short distance across walls.\n<color=yellow>Launch Pads</color> help you jump <color=white>longer distances</color> over obstacles.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/VisionŌöāSecure Vision
GuideBook/Page/Content/Vision/1ŌöāAlways keep an eye out! <color=yellow>Vision</color> helps you make smarter battle decisions, and you and your teammates should always be aware of your surroundings.\n\n<color=yellow>Security Consoles</color> reveal the surrounding area when activated.\n<color=yellow>Glowing Flowers</color> detonate to briefly grant vision in the opposite direction.\n<color=yellow>Telephoto Cameras and Drones</color> can be installed from your inventory to provide vision of their surroundings, so be sure to install them in strategic locations.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/TeamFightŌöāTeam Fight
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamFight/1ŌöāUsing your character's <color=white>Skills, Tactical Skills and Weapon Skills</color> efficiently is the key to winning team fights.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamFight/2Ōöā<color=white>Starting on Day 2</color>, if <color=yellow>you and your teammates</color> are all downed or eliminated, your experiment will be <color=red>terminated</color>.\nIf the enemy team is <color=red>too strong</color>, always consider <color=white>retreating</color> and waiting for a better opportunity.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamFight/3ŌöāWhen a character's HP reaches zero, they enter a <color=yellow>Downed State</color>.\nRight-click on a <color=white>downed ally</color> to revive them.\nEliminate a <color=white>downed enemy</color> to recover some HP and loot their Credits, Consumables, and Food.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/ResurrectionŌöāRevival
GuideBook/Page/Content/Resurrection/1ŌöāStarting from <color=yellow>Day 3</color>, you can use <color=white>Credits</color> at a <color=yellow>Kiosk</color> to revive eliminated allies.\nIf you donŌĆÖt have enough Credits, the revived test subject's Credits will be used automatically.
GuideBook/Page/Content/Resurrection/2ŌöāStarting from <color=yellow>Day 5</color>, <color=white>you can no longer revive allies at the Kiosk.</color>
GuideBook/MainTheme/Title/FINALBATTLEŌöāEndgame
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/BattleReadyŌöāPreparation
GuideBook/Page/Content/BattleReady/1Ōöā<color=yellow>Dr. Wickeline</color> spawns once the end of the match is approaching.\n<color=white>Eliminating</color> her grants you and your teammates a <color=yellow>powerful buff</color>. She also drops <color=yellow>VF Blood Sample</color>, a special material you can use to craft Mythic items.
GuideBook/Page/Content/BattleReady/2ŌöāYou can craft Mythic items using <color=yellow>VF Blood Sample</color> or you can collect them from <color=yellow>Mythic Air Supplies</color>.\nYou can also purchase a <color=yellow>VF Blood Sample</color> from a <color=yellow>Kiosk</color> using your Credits.
GuideBook/Page/Content/BattleReady/3ŌöāPrepare for the endgame by stocking up on <color=white>food and cameras.</color>\nNote: You can also purchase these items through the <color=yellow>V Pad</color><color=white> (Hotkey V)</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/TemporarySafeZoneŌöāTemporary Safe Zones
GuideBook/Page/Content/TemporarySafeZone/1Ōöā<color=yellow>Temporary Safe Zones</color> serve as battlegrounds leading up to the final showdown.\nWhen only three safe areas remain, <color=yellow>the two non-adjacent areas</color> will become temporary safe zones.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TemporarySafeZone/2ŌöāOnly <color=yellow>one team</color> can survive in each temporary safe zone.\nChoose the <color=white>most advantageous battlefield</color> for your team and move accordingly.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TemporarySafeZone/3ŌöāAll <color=yellow>dead or downed teammates</color> of the team that survive the temporary safe zone will be brought back into the experiment.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/LastSafeZoneŌöāFinal Safe Zone
GuideBook/Page/Content/LastSafeZone/1ŌöāOnly the <color=yellow>final battle</color> remains to determine the ultimate winner.\nThe <color=yellow>Chronosphere</color> is designated in the <color=yellow>Final Safe Zone</color>in the area bridging the two temporary safe zones.\nHurry over to the <color=white>Final Safe Zone</color> forming around the Chronosphere!
GuideBook/Page/Content/LastSafeZone/2ŌöāSecure your final victory and claim everything.\n<color=white>Let there be glory to the pawns who reached the end of despair!</color>

Tutorial/Title/Ch1/BasicControls/TitleŌöāControls Guide
Tutorial/Title/Ch1/BasicControls/DescŌöāTry controlling your character using the mouse and keyboard!\n\nYou can <color=#8FDBF8>move</color>, <color=#8FDBF8>select</color>, or <color=#8FDBF8>attack</color> a target with <color=#FFCE2B>Right-click</color>, and use your character's <color=#8FDBF8>Normal Skills</color> with <color=#FFCE2B>Q, W, and E</color>.\n\nPress <color=#FFCE2B>D</color> for the <color=#8FDBF8>Weapon Skill</color> corresponding to your character's weapon type, and <color=#FFCE2B>F</color> for the <color=#8FDBF8>Tactical Skill</color> selected before the experiment began.\n\nPress <color=#FFCE2B>R</color> to unleash your character's finishing move, their <color=#8FDBF8>Ultimate</color>, and deal a powerful blow to your enemies!
Tutorial/Img/Ch2/ItemFarming/TitleŌöāItem Farming
Tutorial/Img/Ch2/ItemFarming/DescŌöāAre you having trouble distinguishing the boxes located on Lumia Island?\nLook for the <color=#8FDBF8>box</color> <color=#8FDBF8>icon</color> to find boxes you haven't checked yet!\n\nNot sure which item to take after opening a box?\nPay attention to items with a <color=#8FDBF8>yellow mark</color>. These are items you must obtain.\n \nDon't know which items you need to collect in your current area?\nDon't worry! The <color=#8FDBF8>Crafting Guide on the right side of the screen</color> shows you the items you can obtain!\n\nSearch only your required materials, so you can craft your targeted equipment!!
Tutorial/Img/Ch3/Route_1/TitleŌöāSaved Plans
Tutorial/Img/Ch3/Route_1/Desc_1ŌöāDon't memorize! Use Saved Plans!\n\nA Saved Plan is essentially your <color=#8FDBF8>farming route</color>. By pre-registering the areas you need to visit and your targeted equipment in a Saved Plan, you can quickly obtain materials without getting lost!\n\nPress <color=#FFCE2B>M</color> to open the <color=#8FDBF8>Expanded Map</color> and check your Saved Plan's route.\n\nIf you're unsure what to do, try crafting items quickly by following your Saved Plan!
Tutorial/Img/Ch3/Route_2/TitleŌöāCollecting Materials
Tutorial/Img/Ch3/Route_2/DescŌöāI know you can't see flowers, stones, or branches in your Saved Plan.\nThat's because these items cannot be obtained from boxes!\n\nYou can collect <color=#8FDBF8>Flowers</color>, <color=#8FDBF8>Stones</color>, and <color=#8FDBF8>Branches</color> from the ground around Lumia Island.\n\nIf you need these items to craft equipment, keep a close eye on your surroundings and collect them!
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/GamePhase/TitleŌöāGame Phases
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/GamePhase/DescŌöāGame phases change according to the time on Lumia Island, bringing gradual changes to the it!\n\nThis includes Day/Night cycles that affect your <color=#8FDBF8>Vision</color> and types of <color=#8FDBF8>Wild Animals</color>, as well as changes to the experiment such as designating restricted areas, spawning boss monsters, and team revival rules.\n\nPaying attention to these changes as the game phase progresses will bring you closer to becoming the final survivor!
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/ItemCalling/TitleŌöāRequesting Items
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/ItemCalling/Desc_1ŌöāIf the material you want isn't in a box, try using a Remote Drone!\n\nYou can have the item you want delivered by a Remote Drone at any time and place by right-clicking it in your targeted item's list!\n\nHowever, keep in mind that you need <color=#8FDBF8>Credits</color> to use a Remote Drone!
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/ItemCalling/Desc_2Ōöā<color=#8FDBF8>Credits</color>, which serve as currency on Lumia Island, can be obtained through various actions.\n\n- First: They are distributed at <color=#8FDBF8>regular intervals</color>.\n- Second: You can get them by killing a <color=#8FDBF8>test subject</color>.\n- Third: You can get them by hunting <color=#8FDBF8>wild animals</color>.
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/NextRoute/TitleŌöāLate-game Items
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/NextRoute/DescŌöāDon't be surprised if a new item list suddenly appears after you've crafted all your targeted items!\n\nYour items list automatically changes to the <color=#8FDBF8>late-game items</color> registered in your Saved Plan.\n\nFarming and crafting the required materials for these newly registered late-game items will bring you closer to becoming the final survivor!
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/SecurityConsole/TitleŌöāSecurity Console
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/SecurityConsole/DescŌöāOperate Security Consoles around the island to gain the most crucial information, vision, and locate your opponents, obtaining a strategic advantage!\n\nOperating a Security Console activates the CCTVs in that area, allowing you to secure vision around the cameras. You also earn a small amount of Credits for operating a Security Console not currently occupied by allies.\n\nConversely, if a CCTV's red light is on and moving toward you, it means someone is watching! Run away immediately!
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/FoodItem/TitleŌöāFood
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/FoodItem/Desc_1ŌöāLow on HP?\n\nTake the food items you've obtained from hunting Wild Animals or from boxes and head to a <color=#8FDBF8>Campfire</color>!\n\nYou can craft high-<color=#8FDBF8>Rarity</color> food at a Campfire using food items.
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/FoodItem/Desc_2ŌöāDid you not find any food items?\n\nDon't worry!\nOn Lumia Island, you can obtain food by collecting <color=#8FDBF8>Potatoes</color>, <color=#8FDBF8>Edible Mushrooms</color> or <color=#8FDBF8>Fishing</color>. Occasionally, you can even find rare food items.\n\nDon't worry if there are no potatoes, edible mushrooms, or fishing spots nearby! You can also obtain food through <color=#8FDBF8>Air Supply Boxes</color>!
Tutorial/Img/Ch7/SpecialObject/TitleŌöāSpecial Objectives
Tutorial/Img/Ch7/SpecialObject/Desc_1ŌöāSpecial objectives spawn all over Lumia Island.\n\nFrom items that can be collected, such as <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color> and <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color>, to <color=#D05EFF>Mythril</color>, <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color>, and <color=#D05EFF>VF Blood Samples</color>!\n\nUse these special materials to craft <color=#FFCE2B>powerful items</color>!
Tutorial/Img/Ch7/SpecialObject/Desc_2ŌöāDoes your targeted item require materials other than <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color> or <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color>?\n\nThen check the minimap. If you see a boss monster, that's your chance to obtain special materials!\n\nKill boss monsters to obtain <color=#D05EFF>Mythril</color>, <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color>, and <color=#D05EFF>VF Blood Samples</color>!
Tutorial/Img/Ch9/SpecialItem/TitleŌöāObtaining Special Materials
Tutorial/Img/Ch9/SpecialItem/Desc_1ŌöāDid you know there are different ways to obtain special materials?\n\nIn addition to collecting Tree of Life and Meteorites or killing specific boss monsters, you can obtain them by <color=#8FDBF8>killing Mutant Wild Animals</color> or <color=#8FDBF8>opening Epic Air Supply Boxes (Purple Boxes)</color>.
Tutorial/Img/Ch9/SpecialItem/Desc_2ŌöāIf you have enough <color=#8FDBF8>Credits</color>, you can also order them through the <color=#8FDBF8>Kiosks</color> located around Lumia Island.\n\nThe amount of <color=#8FDBF8>Credits</color> required varies for each special material. If you fail to get one, find a <color=#8FDBF8>Kiosk</color> and <color=#FFCE2B>order</color> the special material you need!
Tutorial/Img/Ch10/RestrictedArea/TitleŌöāRestricted Areas
Tutorial/Img/Ch10/RestrictedArea/DescŌöāAre you currently in a restricted area <color=#8FDBF8>marked in red on the minimap</color>? If so, get out quickly! Otherwise, the <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> will activate and kill you.\n\nWhen you are in a restricted area, the <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> activates along with an <color=#8FDBF8>ominous effect</color>, and if the <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> reaches 0, your character will immediately <color=#8FDBF8>die</color>.\n\nYou can recover time on your detonation timer by killing other test subjects, but it's always to simply stay out of restricted areas.\n\n"The test subject will explode shortly!ŌĆØ
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/NextRestrictedArea/TitleŌöāRestricted Area Designation
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/NextRestrictedArea/DescŌöāIf you see <color=#8FDBF8>orange</color> in your current area, <color=#8FDBF8>be careful</color>!\nThis means it will <color=#8FDBF8>become a Restricted Area</color> when the next Game Phase begins.\n\nPress <color=#FFCE2B>M</color> right now to open the <color=#8FDBF8>Expanded Map</color> and check your current area.\nIf it's marked in <color=#8FDBF8>orange</color>, it's an upcoming Restricted Area.\n\nThis means it's not dangerous right now, but it will <color=#8FDBF8>become a Restricted Area when the Game Phase changes</color>. Evacuate to a safe area immediately!
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/VLS/TitleŌöāVLS
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/VLS/Desc_1ŌöāThe VLS or vertical launching device does not allow long-distance travel but enables simultaneous movement with teammates through gliding.\n\nIt is also excellent for reconnaissance, as you can see the ground from below a certain altitude while gliding.\n\nOn <color=#8FDBF8>Day 3</color>, Hyperloops in all areas except restricted areas are replaced with vertical launching devices.
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/VLS/Desc_2ŌöāWhen one teammate activates the VLS, that teammate becomes the Host, and other teammates can hop on the device as well.\n\nOnly the Host can control the glide, and other teammates will glide along as companions to the Host. Upon landing, it <color=#8FDBF8>stuns</color> test subjects within the area for a moment, making it useful for initiation.\n\nFrom a certain altitude, you can break free from the current Host teammate and move freely.
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZone/TitleŌöāTemporary Safe Zones
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZone/Desc_1ŌöāWhen the last remaining safe zone is designated as a restricted area, if <color=#8FDBF8>3 or more teams have survived</color>, two temporary safe zones will be created.\n\nWhen a temporary safe zone is <color=#8FDBF8>activated</color>, the area becomes <color=#8FDBF8>isolated</color> from all others. During this time, you are not able to enter or escape.\n\nIf you move out of the temporary safe zone, your <color=#8FDBF8>Detonation Timer</color> is <color=#8FDBF8>triggered</color>. Move to an active Temporary Safe Zone quickly to preserve your <color=#8FDBF8>Detonation Timer</color> and prepare for combat!
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZone/Desc_2ŌöāWhen a temporary safe zone is <color=#8FDBF8>activated</color>, that area is <color=#8FDBF8>isolated</color> from other areas, so be careful.\n\nWhile isolated, you cannot enter that area or escape to others.\n\nMove quickly to an activated temporary safe zone to freeze your <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> and prepare for combat!
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZoneInfo/TitleŌöāTemporary Safe Zones
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZoneInfo/DescŌöāIs your Detonation Timer running out even though you're in a Temporary Safe Zone?\n\nIf <color=#8FDBF8>only your team is located</color> within a Temporary Safe Zone, the Detonation Timer will not activate. However, if <color=#8FDBF8>2 or more teams are located</color> in one Temporary Safe Zone, or if you <color=#8FDBF8>go outside</color> the Temporary Safe Zone, the Detonation Timer will get triggered.\n\nIt's simple: just make sure only your team is in the Temporary Safe Zone! Chase away the enemies and survive!
Tutorial/Img/Ch13/ChronoSphere/TitleŌöāChronosphere
Tutorial/Img/Ch13/ChronoSphere/DescŌöāThe final stage to determine the winner between the last two surviving teams on Lumia Island is the <color=#8FDBF8>Final Safe Zone</color>.\n\nThe Final Safe Zone is created in a <color=#8FDBF8>circle around the Chronosphere</color> spawned in the last restricted area. Your <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> will get triggered in all areas except the safe zone.\n\nThe safe zone will gradually begin to shrink after a certain period, and eventually, all safe zones will disappear, causing your detonation timer to get triggered in all areas. If you don't have much time left on your timer, don't avoid combat with the enemy!
